paper_title: Lilium longiflorum Protein and Pectin Analysis

methods.description: This method describes the process of extracting and purifying proteins from lily stigmas for further analysis.

steps.description: 64 g of frozen stigmas were ground in phosphate-buffered saline, and proteins were precipitated with a stepwise gradient of (NH4)2SO4 (25-90%).
steps.searchable_summary: Grind frozen stigmas and precipitate proteins using ammonium sulfate gradient

steps.description: All precipitates were pooled and dialyzed against ddH2O.
steps.searchable_summary: Dialyze pooled protein precipitates against distilled water

steps.description: The proteins were applied to a CM-Sephadex C-25 cation exchange column and serially eluted with NaCl in MES buffer.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform cation exchange chromatography on proteins

steps.description: Fractions containing SCA were identified by Western blots.
steps.searchable_summary: Identify SCA-containing fractions using Western blot

methods.description: This method involves the purification of SCA proteins using high-performance liquid chromatography (HPLC) for further analysis.

steps.description: SPs were injected into the HPLC column and proteins were eluted with a two-step linear gradient.
steps.searchable_summary: Inject SPs into HPLC and elute proteins with linear gradient

steps.description: Peaks corresponding to SCA were collected, evaporated, and redissolved for mass spectrometric analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect and prepare SCA peaks for mass spectrometry

methods.description: This method describes the extraction and purification of low esterified pectins from lily styles for bioassays.

steps.description: Fresh lily styles were cut and stirred with PAW reagent, then filtered and rinsed.
steps.searchable_summary: Extract pectins from lily styles using PAW reagent

steps.description: Style segments were stirred in imidazole-HCl buffer, filtered, and centrifuged.
steps.searchable_summary: Stir and centrifuge style segments in imidazole-HCl buffer

steps.description: The sample was applied to an anion exchange column and eluted with an imidazole gradient.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform anion exchange chromatography on pectin sample

methods.method_name: In Vitro Pollen Tube Adhesion Assay
methods.description: This assay tests the adhesion of pollen tubes to a matrix of SCA proteins and pectins.

steps.description: HPLC-purified SCA proteins were vacuum-dried and redissolved in water.
steps.searchable_summary: Redissolve vacuum-dried SCA proteins in water

steps.description: SCA/pectin mixtures were incubated and transferred onto nitrocellulose using a dot blotter.
steps.searchable_summary: Transfer SCA/pectin mixtures onto nitrocellulose

steps.description: Pregerminated pollen tubes were laid on the SCA/pectin matrix and incubated.
steps.searchable_summary: Incubate pollen tubes on SCA/pectin matrix

steps.description: Membranes were washed and stained, and the number of bound pollen tubes was counted.
steps.searchable_summary: Count bound pollen tubes after washing and staining

methods.description: This assay evaluates the binding of SCA proteins to pectins under varying ionic strengths.

steps.description: SCA was incubated with stylar pectins and applied to a spin column.
steps.searchable_summary: Incubate SCA with pectins and apply to spin column

steps.description: Proteins released from the pectin matrix were passed through the column by centrifugation.
steps.searchable_summary: Centrifuge to separate proteins from pectin matrix

steps.description: SDS-PAGE and Western blots were used to evaluate pectin-binding SCA.
steps.searchable_summary: Evaluate pectin-binding SCA using SDS-PAGE and Western blot

methods.method_name: Production of Polyclonal Antibodies and Western Blotting
methods.description: This method involves generating polyclonal antibodies against SCA proteins and using them for Western blot analysis.

steps.description: Highly specific antibodies against pure SCA were generated by injection into rabbits.
steps.searchable_summary: Generate polyclonal antibodies by injecting SCA into rabbits

steps.description: Western blotting was used to identify SCA-enriched fractions.
steps.searchable_summary: Identify SCA-enriched fractions using Western blot

methods.description: This method describes the use of ESI-MS to determine the molecular weight of HPLC-purified SCA proteins.

steps.description: Proteins were loaded into the nanoelectrospray tip for ESI-MS analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform ESI-MS analysis on loaded proteins

steps.description: Spectra were processed using deconvolution software to observe monoisotopic mass.
steps.searchable_summary: Process spectra to observe monoisotopic mass

methods.method_name: Peptide Sequence Analysis by Liquid Chromatography-MS/MS
methods.description: This method involves the digestion of SCA proteins and their analysis by liquid chromatography-MS/MS to determine peptide sequences.

steps.description: Proteins were digested with trypsin and injected into an HPLC column for analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: Digest proteins with trypsin and analyze by HPLC-MS/MS

steps.description: Peptides were analyzed to determine sequences using MS/MS.
steps.searchable_summary: Determine peptide sequences using MS/MS

methods.description: This method uses MALDI-TOF mass spectrometry to measure the mono-isotopic m/z values of peptides.

steps.description: Peptides were mixed with matrix and subjected to MALDI analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform MALDI-TOF analysis on peptide-matrix mixture

methods.description: This method involves structural homology modeling of SCA proteins using computational tools.

steps.description: Structural homology modeling for SCA1 and SCA3 was performed using the SWISS-MODEL Web server.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform structural homology modeling using SWISS-MODEL
